title: Intensely Jammy Algerian Digestive Biscuits Tagine
description: A cozy Algerian dish featuring digestive biscuits cooked in a tagine with sweet and spicy flavors, wrapped in pandan leaves for a unique twist. This shojin-friendly recipe is perfect for a cold evening.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 300 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the crushed digestive biscuits, sliced almonds, cinnamon, and ginger.
In a separate bowl, mix the apricot jam and pitted dates.
Add the jam and date mixture to the biscuit mixture and stir until well combined.
Lay a pandan leaf flat on a work surface and place a quarter of the biscuit mixture in the center of the leaf.
Fold the pandan leaf over the filling to form a triangle or square shape, and repeat with the remaining ingredients.
Place the pandan leaf wraps in a large baking dish and drizzle with coconut oil.
Bake the tagine for 25 minutes, or until the pandan leaves are crispy and the filling is warm and jammy.
Serve the intensely jammy Algerian digestive biscuits tagine warm, garnished with additional sliced almonds if desired.
